intro:
Fruitz is a French dating app famous for fruit emojis that signal intent — cherry for serious, grapes for hookups, watermelon for friendship. Light, fun, popular in French cities. Acquired by Bumble in 2022.

Embir shares Fruitz's core insight — intentions should be clear upfront — but applies it with more depth: 6 configurable intent categories, bidirectional orientation filtering, verified profiles, and full LGBTQ+ inclusivity.

sections.title: Fruitz's great idea — taken further
sections.content: Fruitz got one fundamental thing right: clarifying intent before matching avoids misunderstandings. The cherry-for-serious, grapes-for-hookups approach is honest. Embir takes this further: 6 configurable intent categories (serious, casual, friendship, networking, exploration, open), filtering that respects these intents both ways, and verified profiles to make intent declarations credible.
highlights: 6 intents vs 4 fruits — more granular and honest

verdict: Fruitz is well designed for fun, explicit dating in French cities. Embir is the best alternative if you want the same intent clarity with more depth, verification, full LGBTQ+ inclusivity, and free access at launch.
ctaText: Clear intentions, verified profiles — join Embir

faq.q: Is Embir available in France like Fruitz?
faq.a: Yes. France is one of Embir's three primary launch markets.

faq.q: How does Embir handle dating intentions?
faq.a: At signup, you configure your intent from 6 categories. Matching respects intents bidirectionally.

faq.q: Is Embir inclusive for LGBTQ+ users?
faq.a: Yes. Embir was built for all orientations from day one — gay, lesbian, bi, pan, queer, trans, non-binary.
